title: Solar-Farm Inverter Bench: Design a Drop-in Activation for the Inverter-Response Surrogate
strategies: identity / linear head (g(x)=x): the surrogate degenerates to a linear ridge that only sees the linear part of each inverter response; reproduces the evaluator baseline at ~0.1

single modern default (one ReLU / swish): rectified random features are a near-universal basis and clear the linear baseline everywhere, but one shape under-serves the even, localized responses (efficiency bell, MPP parabola) and leaves error on the table

shaped even+localized mixture (abs + gaussian bump): spans both signs and localizes peaks/troughs, generalizing across the visible mix AND the held-out setting; must deliberately AVOID naive periodic (sin) additions that inject high-frequency features and hurt non-periodic settings

over-flexible / mis-scaled activation (e.g. large-gain cube or high-frequency sin stack): either explodes/degenerates the features or overfits the visible mix and generalizes worse than the ReLU default on the held-out setting, so its cross-setting geometric mean regresses toward or below baseline
